mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate collapsing aggregate demand, which forces equity valuations lower over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects expanding corporate output capacity, which supports earnings growth and drives positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to generate positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of post-shock recovery periods, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL exceeds 7.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 3.5 percent imposes high financing costs on leveraged firms and compresses price-to-earnings multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 65% of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5 percent, or the threshold effect vanishes in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which boosts consumer confidence and triggers positive weekly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over a 2-year rolling window, or positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects sustained economic expansion that increases corporate revenue forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic greater than 1.96, or the signal reverses direction during periods of rising inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 2017 baseline index of 100 indicates severe supply-side contraction that restricts output and earnings, causing negative monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO drops below 100, or the threshold effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.
